step_by_step_thinking: The correct answer is B. Penicillin resistance has been well documented for Neisseria gonorrhoeae over decades; hence, it is no longer recommended as first-line therapy. This makes option B false since not all strains are susceptible to penicillin. Option A is true — it's indeed one of the leading causes of urethritis in men. Option C refers correctly to conditions like pustular rash or arthritis due to widespread dissemination (disseminated gonococcal infections). And D is also accurate because this organism only infects humans. Therefore, "all stains are highly susceptible" is incorrect—making B the exception that does NOT hold true.
raw_response:
The correct answer is B. Penicillin resistance has been well documented for Neisseria gonorrhoeae over decades; hence, it is no longer recommended as first-line therapy. This makes option B false since not all strains are susceptible to penicillin. Option A is true — it's indeed one of the leading causes of urethritis in men. Option C refers correctly to conditions like pustular rash or arthritis due to widespread dissemination (disseminated gonococcal infections). And D is also accurate because this organism only infects humans. Therefore, "all stains are highly susceptible" is incorrect—making B the exception that does NOT hold true.

Answer: B
